title: School-scoped reference pickers (no cross-school leak)
business_reason: School create forms had FK ids hard-coded as text (participation_id, exam_slot_id) because the staff lookup is staff-only and a generic lookup would leak other schools' records. Give schools real reference pickers that show ONLY their own data.

design.endpoint: /api/school/lookup/[table] — requireSchoolScope; tables with a school_id are filtered by the authenticated actor.school_id (browser school_id never trusted); exam_slots are resolved through the school's school_exam_slot_assignments; un-scopable tables return empty (never an unscoped list).
design.component: ModuleTable reference fetch is portal-aware — school pages call /api/school/lookup, staff pages /api/staff/lookup.
design.generator: the school registry no longer hard-codes FK text fields (None -> the _ui_fields engine classifies them as reference pickers); school pages additionally hide school_id (the actor's scope, server-set, never a picker).
design.gate: check_no_raw_crud_ui no longer exempts school pages — their FKs must be pickers too. 0 violations.

param_14.question: Do school forms use safe reference pickers?
param_14.answer: Yes. A school selecting a participation, roster batch or exam slot now picks from a dropdown of human labels scoped to its own school (via /api/school/lookup, filtered by the authenticated school_id — never the browser's). exam_slots come through the school's assignments. The school's own school_id is hidden (set server-side), and a school can never see or pick another school's records.
param_14.security_implications: school-scoped: server filters by the authenticated school_id; cross-school options impossible; un-scopable tables return empty rather than leak.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: relationship-scoped lookups for more tables; label search/pagination for large option sets.
